title: Cairo ⇄ Luxor
summary: The full sweep of the Nile aboard a traditional dahabiya under tow — from the Pyramids of Giza through Middle Egypt's hidden monuments to the temple cities of Thebes.
description: A rare twelve-night voyage along the river's least-travelled reaches, linking Cairo with Luxor. Traditional dahabiya cruising, gently towed by a support tugboat for the long passage, with daily international buffet dining and the signature sunset tea on deck.
routeOverview: Twelve nights spanning Middle and Upper Egypt — the pyramids of Giza and Fayoum, the painted tombs of Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera, and the grand monuments of Luxor. Sailable in either direction; northbound (Luxor → Cairo) runs with the current and is noticeably faster.
insights: This is a traditional dahabiya towed by a support tugboat for the long Cairo passage — sailing times are far longer than conventional Nile cruisers, by design, for a slow and authentic journey.
The route reaches Middle Egypt's rarely-visited monuments — Fayoum, Tell el-Amarna, and Beni Hassan — alongside the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera and the great monuments of Thebes.

routes.label: Cairo → Luxor
routes.overview: Experience the timeless beauty of the Nile aboard a traditional dahabiya, elegantly towed by a support tugboat for long-distance navigation. Sailing southbound against the current, the days are unhurried — the point of this slow, authentic river journey.

routes.label: Luxor → Cairo
routes.overview: Sailing northbound from Luxor to Cairo with the Nile current, this direction runs roughly 15–20% faster than the southbound route — beginning amid the grandeur of Thebes and ending at the Pyramids of Giza.

faq.q: Why are the sailing times so long?
faq.a: This is a traditional dahabiya towed by a support tugboat for the long Cairo–Luxor passage. Sailing times are far longer than conventional Nile cruisers — by design. The reward is the slow, authentic river and access to Middle Egypt's rarely-visited monuments.
Northbound (Luxor → Cairo) sails with the Nile current and is roughly 15–20% faster than the southbound Cairo → Luxor route. Both visit the same sites; we tailor the direction to your travel plans.

Very few vessels sail the full Cairo–Luxor reach. You see Giza and the Fayoum pyramids, the painted tombs of Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, and the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera — alongside the great monuments of Thebes.
